verses.text: The sabbath is the first holy convocation. Sabbath. Weekly rest heads the feasts.
The sabbath is the first holy convocation. Sabbath. Weekly rest heads the feasts.
The sabbath is the first holy convocation. Sabbath. Weekly rest heads the feasts.
The passover is on the fourteenth of the first month. Passover. Redemption is remembered.
The passover is on the fourteenth of the first month. Passover. Redemption is remembered.
The feast of unleavened bread follows seven days. Unleavened. Purity follows redemption.
The feast of unleavened bread follows seven days. Unleavened. Purity follows redemption.
The feast of unleavened bread follows seven days. Unleavened. Purity follows redemption.
The sheaf of firstfruits is waved the day after the sabbath. Firstfruits. The harvest's first is given, a type of Christ risen.
The sheaf of firstfruits is waved the day after the sabbath. Firstfruits. The harvest's first is given, a type of Christ risen.
The sheaf of firstfruits is waved the day after the sabbath. Firstfruits. The harvest's first is given, a type of Christ risen.
A lamb is offered with the sheaf; eat no new grain till then. Offered. God's portion comes first.
A lamb is offered with the sheaf; eat no new grain till then. Offered. God's portion comes first.
A lamb is offered with the sheaf; eat no new grain till then. Offered. God's portion comes first.
Fifty days later, Pentecost, with two wave loaves. Pentecost. The full harvest is brought.
Fifty days later, Pentecost, with two wave loaves. Pentecost. The full harvest is brought.
Fifty days later, Pentecost, with two wave loaves. Pentecost. The full harvest is brought.
Offerings accompany it; leave the gleanings for the poor. Generous. Worship and mercy go together.
Offerings accompany it; leave the gleanings for the poor. Generous. Worship and mercy go together.
Offerings accompany it; leave the gleanings for the poor. Generous. Worship and mercy go together.
Offerings accompany it; leave the gleanings for the poor. Generous. Worship and mercy go together.
Offerings accompany it; leave the gleanings for the poor. Generous. Worship and mercy go together.
The first day of the seventh month is a memorial of trumpets. Trumpets. A holy day of blowing.
The first day of the seventh month is a memorial of trumpets. Trumpets. A holy day of blowing.
The first day of the seventh month is a memorial of trumpets. Trumpets. A holy day of blowing.
The tenth day is the day of atonement, of affliction. Atonement. The nation is cleansed.
The tenth day is the day of atonement, of affliction. Atonement. The nation is cleansed.
The tenth day is the day of atonement, of affliction. Atonement. The nation is cleansed.
Whoever is not afflicted that day is cut off; it is a sabbath. Afflicted. The soul humbles itself.
Whoever is not afflicted that day is cut off; it is a sabbath. Afflicted. The soul humbles itself.
Whoever is not afflicted that day is cut off; it is a sabbath. Afflicted. The soul humbles itself.
Whoever is not afflicted that day is cut off; it is a sabbath. Afflicted. The soul humbles itself.
The feast of tabernacles is seven days, dwelling in booths. Tabernacles. They recall the wilderness.
The feast of tabernacles is seven days, dwelling in booths. Tabernacles. They recall the wilderness.
The feast of tabernacles is seven days, dwelling in booths. Tabernacles. They recall the wilderness.
The feast of tabernacles is seven days, dwelling in booths. Tabernacles. They recall the wilderness.
These are the feasts of the LORD, besides the sabbaths. The feasts. God's calendar is full of worship.
These are the feasts of the LORD, besides the sabbaths. The feasts. God's calendar is full of worship.
They keep the feast with branches, rejoicing before God. Rejoicing. Worship is glad.
They keep the feast with branches, rejoicing before God. Rejoicing. Worship is glad.
They keep the feast with branches, rejoicing before God. Rejoicing. Worship is glad.
They dwell in booths, that their children know God led them. Remembered. The story is kept alive.
They dwell in booths, that their children know God led them. Remembered. The story is kept alive.
They dwell in booths, that their children know God led them. Remembered. The story is kept alive.
